overview: This step creates output in a given format using the data extracted from an input document, allowing conversion from one format to another. It takes filter events and sends filter events. The generated documents are **not** meant to be merged back into their original format â€” for round-trip workflows, use the Raw Document to Filter Events Step followed by the Filter Events to Raw Document Step instead.

outputFormat.help:
Select the type of output file to generate. Available formats:

- **PO File** â€” Output in Gettext's PO format, encoded in UTF-8.
- **TMX Document** â€” Output in TMX (Translation Memory eXchange) format.
- **Tab-Delimited Table** â€” Tab-delimited text (UTF-8). First column is source, second is target. Literal tab characters are escaped as `\t`.
- **Pensieve TM** â€” Output to a Pensieve TM database. The output path must be a **directory**, not a file.
- **Parallel Corpus Files** â€” Two plain text files (inline codes stripped), one entry per line. Each file has its locale code appended to the filename. Used with systems like Moses MT.
- **Word Table** â€” RTF table format with generic inline codes. Each entry in a separate row. When input is a TTX file, the match percentage appears in the Type column.

singleOutput.help:
Controls whether all input documents are merged into one output file or each input produces a separate output.

- **Enabled**: A single output document is generated for all input documents. The `outputPath` parameter specifies where to write it.
- **Disabled**: One output document is created per input document. Output paths are determined by the calling application (or by `autoExtensions` if set).
notes: When enabled, the `outputPath` parameter becomes active. When disabled, `autoExtensions` becomes available instead.

outputPath.help:
Full path of the single output document to generate. For **Pensieve TM** output, this must be a **directory** rather than a file path.

The variable `${rootDir}` can be used in the path to reference the root directory of the pipeline.
outputPath.values: File system path. Supports the `${rootDir}` variable.
examples: `${rootDir}/pensieve-db/` â€” for Pensieve TM output (must be a directory)

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this field to be active

autoExtensions.help: When set, output file paths are constructed by appending the format-specific extension (`.po`, `.tmx`, etc.) to each input file's path. If not set, output paths are determined by the calling application.

dependsOn.condition: must be false (disabled) for this option to be available

targetStyle.help: Controls what content is placed in the target side of each output entry.

useGenericCodes.help:
Replaces original inline codes with a generic numbered format: `<N>` for opening, `</N>` for closing, and `<N/>` for standalone codes, where `N` is the code's identifier.

When disabled, inline codes are output in their original format (except for references).
notes: If the output format is **TMX**, this option is ignored â€” inline codes always use the TMX format regardless of this setting.

skipEntriesWithoutText.help: Filters out entries from the input document that have no actual text content â€” i.e., entries that are empty or contain only inline codes with no surrounding text.

approvedEntriesOnly.help: Restricts output to entries where the target has the `approved` property set to `yes`. For example, "fuzzy" entries in a PO file have this property set to `no` and would be excluded.
notes: Entries that do not have the `approved` property at all are treated as **non-approved** and will be excluded when this option is enabled.

overwriteSameSource.help:
When importing into a Pensieve TM, if an entry has the same source text as one or more existing TM entries, all matching existing entries are **replaced** by the new one.

Leave disabled if you want to allow multiple different translations for the same source text.

dependsOn.condition: only applicable when output format is Pensieve TM

processingNotes: Output documents generated by this step are **not** designed for round-trip merging back into the original format. For round-trip workflows, use the Raw Document to Filter Events Step and Filter Events to Raw Document Step instead.
For **Pensieve TM** output, the `outputPath` must point to a directory, not a file.
**Tab-Delimited Table** output escapes literal tab characters as `\t`.
**Parallel Corpus Files** output strips all inline codes and appends locale codes to filenames.
**Word Table** output shows match percentages in the Type column when the input is a TTX file.

examples.title: Convert XLIFF to TMX (single output)
examples.description: Merge all input documents into a single TMX file, skipping empty and unapproved entries.
examples.config:
```yaml
outputFormat: tmx
singleOutput: true
outputPath: ${rootDir}/output/translations.tmx
skipEntriesWithoutText: true
approvedEntriesOnly: true
```

examples.title: Convert to PO files (per-input output)
examples.description: Generate one PO file per input document, with the `.po` extension appended to each input path.
examples.config:
```yaml
outputFormat: po
singleOutput: false
autoExtensions: true
skipEntriesWithoutText: true
```

examples.title: Export to Pensieve TM with deduplication
examples.description: Write entries to a Pensieve TM database directory, replacing existing entries that share the same source text.
examples.config:
```yaml
outputFormat: pensieve
singleOutput: true
outputPath: ${rootDir}/pensieve-db/
overwriteSameSource: true
```
